name: gdb
title: gdb
summary: gcore - Generate core files for running processes
description:
GNU Debugger
GDB is a source-level debugger, capable of breaking programs at
any specific line, displaying variable values, and determining
where errors occurred. Currently, gdb supports C, C++, D,
Objective-C, Fortran, Java, OpenCL C, Pascal, assembly, Modula-2,
Go, and Ada. A must-have for any serious programmer.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use gcore because gcore - Generate core files for running processes.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use gcore as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `gcore [-a] [-o filename] pid[s]` as documented upstream for gcore.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair gcore with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `gcore --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
